commit.message:
fix: Telegram bot improvements — inspect, dedup, context (#54)

* fix: handle Anthropic tool_use finish_reason in agent loop

The agent loop checked for finish_reason == "tool_calls" (OpenAI
format) but Anthropic returns "tool_use". This caused the loop to
break after round 1 without processing tool results, producing
empty responses ("no response generated").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add /inspect command to Telegram bot

Shows tool actions, sources, and timing from the last query.
Also captures statuses, sources, and perf_trace events from
the chat SSE stream for inspection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate Telegram messages from Claude Code sessions

[NOTIFY] messages were sent during assistant events AND re-extracted
from the result event, causing duplicates. Now skips re-extraction
when notifications were already sent during the session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: detect imperative follow-ups for conversation context retention

"look in my email", "check my calendar", "search drive for it" etc.
were not recognized as follow-ups, so the previous question's context
was lost. Added regex pattern for imperative verbs + data source nouns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add tests for PR review findings; strip PII from /inspect

- Add unit tests for expand_followup_query imperative patterns
- Add tests for orchestrator notification dedup (notifications_sent > 0)
- Move _IMPERATIVE_FOLLOWUP_RE to module level per convention
- Strip tool input args from /inspect output (show tool name only)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: universal stock search - all KOSPI/KOSDAQ/US/ETF stocks

Replace hardcoded stock DB with dynamic search:

1. New /api/stock/search endpoint:
   - Yahoo Finance search for US stocks, global ETFs, Korean stocks by code
   - Comprehensive Korean name DB (80+ stocks: banks, Samsung, SK, LG, bio,
     defense, entertainment, semiconductor, ETFs etc.)
   - Automatic Korean/English query detection

2. Frontend search improvements:
   - Debounced API search (300ms) + instant local DB search
   - Results merged (local + API, deduplicated)
   - Direct symbol input: type "024110" -> auto-adds .KS suffix
   - Any valid symbol works even if not in DB (AAPL, SPY, QQQ, etc.)

3. Supports all markets:
   - KOSPI/KOSDAQ stocks (Korean name + code search)
   - All US stocks (NYSE, NASDAQ)
   - Korean ETFs (KODEX, TIGER, etc.)
   - US ETFs (SPY, QQQ, VOO, etc.)
   - Global stocks and indices

feat: accelerate cadence to machine speed — hourly scans, daily targets

Cortex is AI — it should run at AI speed, not human speed.

- Scan tools, releases, own projects: hourly (was daily)
- Scan strategies, industry: daily (was weekly)
- Review adoptions: daily (was weekly)
- All source frequencies updated to match
- Added daily 10% improvement target with scoreboard
- Updated meta-principles: zero-improvement days are failures
- Compounding mindset: 1% daily = 37x yearly

commit.message:
fix: remove floating WhatsApp button from blog pages

Blog articles already have inline CTA buttons — float is redundant.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: adoption lifecycle — plan before adopting, nag until decided

Adoptions now follow a lifecycle:
  evaluate → plan → pending-approval → active → review → improve/ditch

Key changes:
- "Adopt" recommendation triggers a planning phase, not immediate action
- Adoption plans go to tracking/adoption-plans/[id].md with time
  estimates, risks, hypotheses with confidence levels, and rollback
- Items in pending-approval are re-surfaced every review cycle
- Items pending >7 days get escalated in digests
- Items pending >14 days trigger a direct approve/reject/defer prompt
- Pending items never silently disappear

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: missing stocks + SMA200 on intraday/short timeframes

1. Add 15+ missing Korean stocks (IBK, Woori, Hana, SKT, KT, LG Electronics, etc.)
   to both STOCK_SEARCH_DB and TICKER_TO_KOREAN_NAME

2. Fix SMA200 (200-day moving average) not showing on 1D/1W/1M timeframes:
   - Problem: intraday charts only had 5min/15min candles, so SMA(200)
     calculated 200 candles, not 200 DAYS
   - Solution: fetch 2 years of daily data separately (sma200 timeframe)
     and calculate true 200-day SMA, then map by date to intraday chart
   - For 3M+ timeframes, continue using chart data directly (enough points)

fix: larger embers, slower float, accent glow at bottom edge

Ember sizes ~2x larger (5-17px glow, 3-5px sparks, was 3-9px/2px).
Float duration ~2x slower (5-10s, was 2.5-5s). Added a faint
radial gradient glow at the bottom of the container in the accent
color — peeks up from the edge for ambient atmosphere.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Navigate to dashboard instantly on sign-in, auth completes in background

Firebase Auth can take several seconds to verify credentials. Instead of
blocking the user on the login page during this wait, navigate to the
dashboard immediately and show a loading spinner there while auth finishes.

A sessionStorage flag (authPending) signals the dashboard to hold its
redirect guard open during the in-flight auth request, preventing a
premature redirect back to login.

commit.message:
Fix login redirect loop causing infinite spinner

After signInWithEmailAndPassword resolves, auth.currentUser is set
synchronously but onAuthStateChanged fires async. The dashboard was
seeing user=null and immediately redirecting back to login, trapping
the user in a loop with a frozen spinner.

- Dashboard layout now checks auth.currentUser before redirecting to
  login, preventing the premature redirect during the auth state sync gap
- Dashboard shows a loading spinner while auth state propagates to React context
- Login page navigates immediately after auth resolves via router.push

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs(tasks): correct Epic 19 research — thorough piper_tts evaluation and model format details

Previous research dismissed piper_tts too quickly and had gaps in model
architecture/quantization analysis. This corrects with thorough evaluation:

- piper_tts (v0.0.1): API documented (Piper.modelPath, generateSpeech),
  backed by babylon.cpp/DeepPhonemizer, but Android broken per changelog,
  GitHub repo 404, no iOS, abandoned 23mo
- piper_tts_plugin (v0.0.2): newer active project with voice packs, but
  only 8 commits, no iOS, bundled packs only
- sherpa-onnx remains the clear recommendation (9.1k/wk downloads, iOS,
  custom model support)
- Corrected VITS architecture details per quality level from Piper source
  (hidden_channels, decoder config)
- Clarified that no pre-quantized Piper models exist upstream (FP32 only)
- Added quantization caveats for VITS decoder/vocoder layers

commit.message:
v0.7.0 — audit enhancements, brew-upgrade, apply-defaults

security-audit.sh:
  - Bluetooth state check via system_profiler (finding: Medium)
  - Developer mode check via DevToolsSecurity (finding: Medium)
  - World-readable files scan: ~/.ssh, ~/.aws, ~/.gnupg, ~/.config/op (finding: High)
  - Sudoers NOPASSWD detection (finding: High)
  - New sections: Sensitive File Permissions, Sudoers Configuration

brew-upgrade.sh (mac-deploy-upgrade):
  - brew update → captures outdated list → brew upgrade → brew cleanup
  - Logs dated upgrade summary to private/machines/<hostname>/brew-upgrades.log
  - --dry-run shows outdated packages without installing
  - --no-casks skips cask upgrades

apply-defaults.sh (mac-deploy-defaults):
  - Screen lock: require password immediately, 5-minute screensaver
  - Screenshots: move to ~/Documents/Screenshots, disable shadows
  - Finder: show extensions, hidden files, full path in title bar
  - AirDrop: contacts only
  - Mail: disable remote image loading (tracking pixel protection)
  - Safari: no auto-open downloads, fraud warnings, full URL bar
  - Software Update: auto-check, auto-download, security data (sudo)
  - --dry-run prints each defaults write command without applying

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
docs(tasks): complete Epic 19 discovery — local Piper TTS feasibility research

Research findings for on-device Piper TTS via sherpa-onnx Flutter package:
- sherpa-onnx v1.12.28 confirmed as integration path (mature, 9.1k/wk downloads)
- Model size is 63MB (not 18MB as estimated) — revised to download-on-first-use
- NNAPI crashes for TTS models — CPU-only is the only viable provider
- ~500MB peak RAM during inference — memory is the primary risk
- RTF ~0.15-0.40 estimated for Android (faster than real-time)
- INT8 quantization can reduce model to ~22MB with minimal quality loss
- Server-side TTS fallback artifacts already exist (tts-fallback-monitor.ts)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Add in-memory TTL cache service for frequently accessed data

- Add pagination to conversations endpoint (50 default, 100 max)
- Batch queued message delivery in socket handler (10 per batch)
- Add cache invalidation on block/unblock operations
- Use cached blocking utils in messages controller

commit.message:
Define engine telemetry observability contract with deterministic sampling and sketch bounds (RGC-066A)

Implement the RGC-066A observability channel policy: ObservabilityMode
(5 operator-visible modes with precedence ordering), SketchFamily
(5 approximate sketch types), PayloadFamily classification,
TelemetrySitePolicy matrix, TelemetrySamplingContract with deterministic
seed derivation from trace/workload/manifest/site identity, and
SketchErrorEnvelope bounds for bias/variance/collision/quantile error.

Add canonical policy functions producing full contracts, replay fixture
matrix proving schedule reproducibility, machine-readable JSON contract,
markdown documentation, and CI replay scripts.

Update .beads/issues.jsonl with tracking entries for all new work items.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: franken_engine
repository.description: Native Rust runtime for adversarial extension workloads with deterministic replay, cryptographic decision receipts, and fleet-scale containment.

commit.message:
Add TS module resolution index, ESM/CJS evidence harnesses, native addon membrane, and regime signatures

Five new modules registered in lib.rs:

ts_module_resolution (RGC-406A): ART/MPHF package index structures for
zero-IO module resolution with stable error codes (FE-TSRES-IDX-0001..0007),
validation reports, and index manifest schemas.

esm_cjs_interop_parity (RGC-309B): Evidence harness verifying live-binding,
async-evaluation, and mixed-graph interop at the module infrastructure level.
Follows corpus/runner/inventory/bundle pattern with deterministic artifacts.

esm_cjs_parity_evidence (RGC-309C): Execution parity evidence through the
orchestrator pipeline including format detection, inter-format interop, and
cycle handling with deterministic artifacts.

native_addon_membrane (RGC-407): Capability-safe Node-API and native-addon
membrane planning — inventories addon cohorts, derives ABI fingerprints,
plans capability-membrane/fallback/rejection routing.

regime_signature_feature (RGC-617A): Lifts raw runtime traces into bounded-
dimensional signature vectors using deterministic hash-bucketing. Regime
classification with Abstention label for noisy/short traces. Fixed-point
millionths arithmetic throughout.

All modules include comprehensive integration test suites.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: franken_engine
repository.description: Native Rust runtime for adversarial extension workloads with deterministic replay, cryptographic decision receipts, and fleet-scale containment.

commit.message:
Optimize stdlib string builtins with ASCII fast paths and single-pass UTF-16 metrics

Replace multiple independent string-inspection passes (utf16_code_units,
has_non_bmp_scalars, chars().count()) with a single-pass inspect_string()
that returns all StringMetrics at once. Add ASCII fast paths to charAt,
charCodeAt, codePointAt, indexOf, lastIndexOf, search, slice, and
utf16_slice_lossless — avoiding full Vec<u16> materialization for
ASCII-only strings. Introduce utf16_code_unit_at() and
utf16_offset_for_match() helpers for targeted code-unit access without
full collection. Add unit tests for non-BMP surrogate behavior and a
4096-iteration ASCII hot-loop benchmark integration test with artifact
reporting.

Addresses RGC-312A.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: franken_engine
repository.description: Native Rust runtime for adversarial extension workloads with deterministic replay, cryptographic decision receipts, and fleet-scale containment.

commit.message:
Implement short-circuit lowering for logical compound member-assignment operators

Lower &&=, ||=, and ??= on member expressions (both static and computed
properties) through proper short-circuit branch ops (JumpIfTruthy,
JumpIfFalsyConsume, JumpIfNullish) instead of rejecting them as
unsupported frontier expressions. The generated IR evaluates the object
and computed key exactly once, reads the current property value, branches
conditionally, and writes back the RHS only when the short-circuit
condition is met. Add integration tests covering all three operators
with both static and computed property access patterns, plus lowering
coverage integration tests.

Addresses RGC-307A1.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: franken_engine
repository.description: Native Rust runtime for adversarial extension workloads with deterministic replay, cryptographic decision receipts, and fleet-scale containment.

commit.message:
Fix flow envelope confidence to count only ablation trials, excluding statically-safe flows

Change FlowEnvelopeSynthesizer confidence interval computation so n_trials
counts only flows subjected to ablation (removable flows), and n_essential
counts only flows promoted by the oracle minus those already statically
required. Previously the confidence ratio included statically-safe flows
in the denominator, inflating apparent confidence. Update all affected
test expectations in flow_envelope, flow_envelope_edge_cases, and
flow_envelope_integration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
